title: Knight Probability in Chessboard
prompt:
You are given an n x n chessboard. A knight starts at (row, col) and makes exactly k moves. Each move is chosen uniformly at random from the 8 possible knight moves.

Return the probability that the knight remains on the board after k moves.

Return the exact value without rounding.
constraintQuestions: Are moves only standard knight moves? (Yes.)
Should we return 0 if the knight starts off the board? (Yes.)

walkthrough.title: DP state
walkthrough.body:
Let dp[steps][r][c] be the probability that the knight is on (r, c) after exactly steps moves.
We build from steps = 0 up to k.

walkthrough.title: Transition
walkthrough.body:
For each cell at step s, add its probability to the cells it can reach at step s + 1.
Each move contributes dp[s][r][c] / 8 to a reachable cell.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(k * n^2 * 8) which is O(k * n^2).
Space: O(n^2) for two boards.
